pattern: The cheapest way into a cell is its own cost plus the cheaper of the two cells that can lead into it, so a single sweep accumulates the optimum.
hints: You can only arrive at a cell from directly above or directly to its left.
The best total to reach a cell is grid[i][j] plus the smaller of the best totals of those two predecessors.
Fill a table top-left to bottom-right; the first row and column have only one predecessor (a running prefix sum), and every other cell is grid[i][j] + min(up, left).

edge_inputs.description: three by three grid from the statement
single cell is its own cost
a cheap detour beats the diagonal-looking route
all zeros give a zero cost path

stress.description: large random non-negative grid up to the size cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 200)
    n = rng.randint(1, 200)
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 200) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: values and dimensions stay within the constraints
